title: Joe and Michael Touch base
summary: Michael Donovan and Joe Furnari discussed website cleanup, reconciling content, addressing Vinnie's feedback on wordy legacy pages, and navigation changes. They also covered FAQ updates and decided against a pre-NADA website launch, opting for a post-NADA 'awesome splash'.

decisions.description: Focus on updating new pages and hiding excessive legacy content.
Replace the current FAQ section with Vinnie's new list of eight questions.
Do not launch a new website before the NADA event due to risk.
Reposition 'resources' and 'lead sources' categories to other sections.
Remove 'leads' and 'lead magnets' and rely on a form short-term.

actionItems.task: Reconcile the content list, including Vinnie's updates.
Surface a list of 30 legacy pages for Vinnie's validation to hide.
Reposition 'resources' and 'lead sources' to other categories.
Replace the existing FAQ with Vinnie's new list of eight questions.
Share the link to the original FAQ page with Joe Furnari via Slack.
Get confirmation from Vinnie on preferred FAQ version by tomorrow morning.
Connect with Michael Donovan mid-morning tomorrow.
Review the FAQ content for spelling issues and clarity.
Consolidate pages under 'insights' and 'resources' if appropriate.

tensions: Vinnie's disappointment with wordy legacy pages from Ken and Honest.
Michael's frustration over squandered time on website improvements.
Disagreement on the timing of a new website launch before NADA.
Company's lack of a proven track record for delivering on tight timelines.
The challenge of reconciling Vinnie's changing FAQ requirements.
referencesToPast: Vinnie's feedback from last week regarding wordy pages.

contributions.description: Proposed focusing on new pages and hiding legacy content instead of refreshing all.
Proposed surfacing a list of 30 legacy pages for Vinnie's validation to hide.
Suggested moving 'AI intelligent engine' under 'BDC solutions' for two stacks.
Proposed removing 'leads' and 'lead magnets' and relying on a form short-term.
Suggested replacing the previously built FAQ with Vinnie's new list directly.
Clarified Vinnie's expectation for consistent look and feel across drop-downs.
Proposed bringing in outside help to finish content rewriting before NADA.
Strongly advised against launching a new website before NADA due to risk.
Explained the need for 'snippets' and 'chunked down' content for search engines.
Recommended an 'awesome splash' launch of the new site after the NADA event.
